source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

We’re looking for senior software engineers to help us realize TruckSmarter’s vision of eliminating inefficiencies across the freight industry. You’ll be at the forefront of building text- and voice-based AI agents that can drive external communication, perform reasoning, execute actions, and continuously self-improve.

As a senior engineer, you’ll play a critical role not only in building systems but also in shaping our technical and product strategy. You’ll mentor peers, influence architectural decisions, and help us define what best-in-class AI-driven logistics software looks like.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Partner closely with product, design, and leadership to define and evolve the architecture for agentic AI systems that scale across multiple product surfaces.

 - Own the design and delivery of complex features end-to-end, balancing tradeoffs between velocity, scalability, and long-term maintainability.

 - Lead technical discussions and architectural reviews, ensuring our systems remain robust, extensible, and secure.

 - Guide the integration of GenAI agents with traditional ML systems (classification, recommendation, optimization) to create hybrid intelligent workflows.

 - Establish engineering best practices around testing, observability, deployment, and performance monitoring for AI-driven features.

 - Proactively identify product and infrastructure bottlenecks, propose innovative solutions, and drive them to completion.

 - Mentor and coach other engineers, raising the technical bar of the team.

 - Stay ahead of emerging AI frameworks and technologies, evaluating and introducing them when they create real product advantage.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of industry experience, including leading projects end-to-end.

 - Demonstrated ability to design, build, and ship complex, production-grade systems.

 - Extremely curious about LLMs and Agentic AI architectures.

 - Experience in fast-paced, startup environments where you’ve had to wear multiple hats.

 - A track record of mentoring engineers and shaping team practices.

 - Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (or equivalent practical experience).




WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.

 


WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits

 

We’d love for you to join us on this journey and are excited to review your application!

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Platform Engineer to build and own the foundation that everything at TruckSmarter runs on. This is a high-ownership role on a team of fewer than 15 engineers where you'll architect core systems, drive developer velocity, and build infrastructure that supports both our product and our growing AI workloads.

You'll work across the full infrastructure stack, from defining deployment pipelines and observability frameworks to leading security initiatives and mentoring engineers on reliability and performance. At our size, your decisions have immediate, visible impact on the product and the team.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and evolve our core platform architecture, including application frameworks and abstractions powering TruckSmarter's systems

 - Build and scale the infrastructure supporting our traditional ML and agentic AI systems

 - Build and maintain reliable infrastructure across AWS (ECS Fargate, RDS, S3, WAF, Redshift, DMS) using IaC (CDK, Terraform)

 - Define and automate deployment pipelines, CI/CD, and observability frameworks

 - Lead security initiatives to ensure compliance, harden systems, and improve access controls

 - Drive incident response and postmortem culture to increase resilience and operational excellence

 - Mentor engineers on best practices in reliability, automation, and performance engineering
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years building and scaling production infrastructure

 - Strong background in AWS (ECS, RDS, VPC, S3, IAM, WAF) and IaC (CDK, CloudFormation, Terraform, or similar)

 - Comfortable with TypeScript and Node.js or similar backend ecosystems

 - Proven ability to lead projects end-to-end and mentor peers

 - Experience thriving in fast-moving startup environments where you've had to wear multiple hats

 - Demonstrated experience in lieu of a formal degree is absolutely fine
   
   


WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.

 


WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits

 

We would love for you to join us on this journey and are excited to review your application!

source_jobs.title: Staff Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a Staff Platform Engineer to build and own the foundation that everything at TruckSmarter runs on. This is a high-ownership, high-impact role on a team of fewer than 15 engineers where you'll define and evolve core systems, drive company-wide developer velocity, and architect infrastructure that supports both our product and our growing AI workloads.

You'll operate across the full infrastructure stack, from setting direction on deployment pipelines and observability frameworks to leading security and reliability strategy and mentoring engineers across the team. At our size, your decisions set long-term technical direction and have immediate, visible impact on both the product and how the team operates.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Define the architectural direction for our core platform, including application frameworks, abstractions, and the systems that every engineer at TruckSmarter depends on

 - Set the standard for how we build, deploy, and operate software across the engineering org

 - Build and maintain reliable infrastructure across AWS (ECS Fargate, RDS, S3, WAF, Redshift, DMS) using IaC (CDK, Terraform)

 - Define and automate deployment pipelines, CI/CD, and observability frameworks

 - Lead security initiatives to ensure compliance, harden systems, and improve access controls

 - Drive incident response and postmortem culture to increase resilience and operational excellence

 - Mentor engineers across the team on reliability, automation, and performance — not just within platform




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 7+ years building and scaling production infrastructure, including setting architectural direction for an engineering org

 - Deep background in AWS (ECS, RDS, VPC, S3, IAM, WAF) and IaC (CDK, CloudFormation, Terraform, or similar)

 - Comfortable with TypeScript and Node.js or similar backend ecosystems

 - Proven ability to establish foundational platform decisions that serve as a basis for other engineers' development

 - Demonstrated ability to lead and influence across teams without relying on org structure

 - Experience at a fast-moving startup where you've had to balance long-term architecture with short-term velocity

 - Demonstrated experience in lieu of a formal degree is absolutely fine




WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.




WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits



We would love to have you be a part of something special, and are excited to review your application.

source_jobs.title: Brand Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

TruckSmarter is hiring a Brand Designer to help re-build and scale our brand as we roll out a new wave of AI-powered products.

This is a hands-on, high-ownership role with real room to grow. You'll work closely with our design manager and the broader leadership team to bring TruckSmarter's brand to life across product, marketing, and everything in between. Your work will ship fast and show up in front of real customers.

We're a small, cross-functional crew of designers, product managers, engineers, and operators. We move quickly, collaborate tightly, and care deeply about craft, but we care just as much about getting things out the door. If you like scrappy teams, real responsibility, and building something that actually makes customers' lives better, you'll feel right at home here.




WHAT EXCITES YOU

 - Helping bring TruckSmarter brand to life across every touchpoint, including web, social, ads, email, events, and product surfaces

 - Designing alongside a small team where your work ships in days, not quarters

 - Owning projects end-to-end, from first sketch to final asset, while learning from senior designers along the way

 - Stretching across disciplines: visual identity, marketing creative, motion, web, events, and the occasional t-shirt

 - Caring about the small stuff like kerning, color, hierarchy, and the weird detail nobody else notices

 - Helping evolve our brand system as it grows, not just executing within it




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 4+ years of digital design experience, ideally at a startup or in-house creative team

 - A portfolio that shows range and taste across visual identity, marketing creative, digital advertising, and web

 - A startup mindset–you're at home with ambiguity, eager to build, and not precious about pitching in with high priority initiatives

 - Strong working knowledge of Figma and Adobe Creative Suite

 - Curiosity about how AI is changing creative workflows, and excitement about using it

 - Familiarity with Framer or other similar web development tools

 - Experience creating content inside lifecycle marketing platforms like customer.io

 - Motion chops using After Effects, LottieFiles, or similar tools




WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.

 


WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits

 

We would love for you to join us on this journey and are excited to review your application!

source_jobs.title: Mobile Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

We’re looking for an experienced mobile engineer to help us transform TruckSmarter’s new vision into a reality for all the freight industry. In this role, you’ll:

 - Design and implement secure, scalable, and high-performance mobile applications for iOS and/or Android platforms using React Native (with support to learn React Native on the job).

 - Provide guidance on mobile app best practices, coding standards, and new technologies.

 - Review code and offer constructive feedback to peers to ensure high-quality deliverables.

 - Work closely with product managers, UX/UI designers, and backend engineers to align on requirements and deliver solutions.

 - Design frontend deployment pipelines balancing velocity and reliability to help frontend engineers build excellent product experiences for our users.

 - Make crucial architectural decisions that will impact feature development for years to come.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Owning large surface area within our product & see things go from 0 to 1.

 - Comfort in ambiguous situations. You'll be responsible for scaffolding out new web services and deciding on what tools and frameworks we will use moving forward.

 - Working across the entire product lifecycle from conceptualization through production.

 - A collaborative startup where you have full autonomy over your work and can make direct impact on users.

 - Solving problems with pragmatic yet sophisticated solutions—you know how to strive for simplicity without compromising the more complex use cases.

 - Comfortable working directly with designers across product and brand.

 - Work cross-functionally with the team to shape the product from strategy, definition to execution, validation, and future iterations.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3 - 7 years of experience building frontend applications that are used by a significant number of users.

 - Proficiency with technologies such as React Native/Flutter, React, and Next.js.

 - Experience with mobile design patterns (MVC, MVP, MVVM, VIPER), modern architecture, and best practices.

 - Prior experience working in a fast-paced, startup environment.

 - Excellent written and oral communication skills and the capability to drive cross-functional requirements with business, product, operations and engineering teams.

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.
   
   


WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.




WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits



We would love for you to join us on this journey and are excited to review your application!

source_jobs.title: Senior Mobile Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Mobile Engineer to own the mobile experience inside Dispatch. This is a high-ownership role on a team of fewer than 15 engineers where you'll shape architecture, drive technical decisions, and ship features that real truck drivers depend on every day.

You won't be handed a backlog and told to execute. You'll work closely with product and design from concept through launch, influence how the mobile team builds, and help set the technical bar for what we ship.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and implement secure, scalable, high-performance mobile applications using React Native

 - Make architectural decisions that will shape how we build the mobile product for years to come

 - Own frontend deployment pipelines that balance velocity and reliability

 - Review code and provide high-leverage feedback to peers

 - Work directly with product managers and designers to align on requirements and deliver polished user experiences

 - Provide guidance on mobile best practices, coding standards, and emerging technologies
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years building and shipping mobile applications used by a significant number of real users

 - Strong iOS or Android engineering fundamentals and a track record of shipping quality mobile products

 - React Native experience is a plus but not required — strong iOS or Android engineers are encouraged to apply and we'll support you in getting up to speed

 - Solid understanding of mobile architecture patterns and modern best practices

 - Experience making principled technical tradeoffs in fast-moving startup environments

 - Ability to work cross-functionally and drive requirements across product, design, and engineering

 - Strong communication skills, written and verbal




WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.




WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits



We would love for you to join us on this journey and are excited to review your application!

source_jobs.title: Staff AI Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TRUCKSMARTER

Trucking powers the American economy, moving 71% of the nation’s freight and representing nearly $900 billion in annual spend. Yet despite its scale, the industry still runs on fragmented systems, phone calls, emails, tribal knowledge, and manual coordination. TruckSmarter is changing that, starting with the people at the center of it all: truck drivers and small carriers.

We’re a team of fewer than 25 building Dispatch, an AI-powered operating layer for freight. Think Claude Code for trucking. Dispatch helps carriers navigate a constantly changing freight market, reason through complex tradeoffs, and execute operational workflows on their behalf.
The product is live, drivers are using it, and we’re now focused on making Dispatch dramatically smarter and more capable.




THE ROLE

This is a high-ownership, high-influence engineering role on a team of fewer than 15 engineers. You'll report to the Head of Engineering and work directly with our CEO on the most technically ambitious work we're doing.

With our small team, there's no bureaucracy between you and impact. You'll define how we architect and scale our core systems, mentor engineers who want to learn from you, and ship software that real truck drivers depend on every day.

For those currently at a larger organization, this role offers significantly increased scope, more challenging problems, and a direct influence on product strategy.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Partner with product, design, and engineering to turn ambiguous problems into clear technical plans and scalable architectures

 - Own end-to-end design and delivery of complex, cross-cutting features across product, infrastructure, reliability, and AI-powered systems.

 - Define and evolve the architecture for core systems and AI platforms that are fast, resilient, and built to scale

 - Raise the bar for quality, observability, performance, and iteration

 - Make architectural decisions that influence how we build for years, balancing speed with long-term maintainability

 - Lead technical reviews and give high-leverage feedback through code reviews, design docs, and system-level guidance

 - Mentor and unblock engineers; help the team move faster by improving systems, tooling, and execution patterns
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 8+ years building and shipping production software, including leading large projects end-to-end

 - A track record of architecting scalable systems and making principled tradeoffs across product, reliability, and velocity

 - Demonstrated ability to mentor engineers and influence without relying on org structure

 - Comfort navigating fast-moving environments with shifting priorities and imperfect information
   
   


WORKING MODEL

We work in-person 5 days a week at our downtown San Francisco office. We believe the best products get built when people are in the same room. Fast feedback loops, real collaboration, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. If that's how you do your best work, you'll fit right in.




WE OFFER

 - Full health, dental and vision coverage

 - Daily catered lunch provided in-office, plus dinner covered for anyone working past 6:00pm

 - Commuter benefits

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401k

 - Commuter benefits



We’d love for you to join us on this journey and are excited to review your application!

notes: Highly technical roles, AI-powered product, and small team size indicate a strong engineering focus.
